BUILD.bazel.content:
load("//kernel:fmt.bzl", "fmt_test")
load("//kernel:tagged.bzl", "tagged_file")

# All git-tracked files in this package must be registered with Bazel.
# bin/other is the fork's namesake-CLI shim (AIDR-00141 Stage 3.5d);
# bin/defn rides via the kernel bundle as a compat alias that also
# resolves to the active tenant's CLI through `mise run defn`.
exports_files([
    "bazel-runner",
    "bbs",
    "bootstrap-bazelrc",
    "defn",
    "k3k",
    "other",
    "yae",
])

fmt_test(name = "build_bazel_fmt", src = "BUILD.bazel", tool = "buildifier")
fmt_test(name = "bbs_fmt", src = "bbs", tool = "shfmt")
fmt_test(name = "yae_fmt", src = "yae", tool = "shfmt")
fmt_test(name = "bazel_runner_fmt", src = "bazel-runner", tool = "shfmt")
fmt_test(name = "bootstrap_bazelrc_fmt", src = "bootstrap-bazelrc", tool = "shfmt")
fmt_test(name = "defn_fmt", src = "defn", tool = "cljstyle")
fmt_test(name = "k3k_fmt", src = "k3k", tool = "shfmt")
fmt_test(name = "other_fmt", src = "other", tool = "cljstyle")

tagged_file(name = "build_bazel_tag", src = "BUILD.bazel", tags = ["bazel-build"])
tagged_file(name = "bazel_runner_tag", src = "bazel-runner", tags = ["script", "shell"])
tagged_file(name = "bootstrap_bazelrc_tag", src = "bootstrap-bazelrc", tags = ["script", "shell"])
tagged_file(name = "bbs_tag", src = "bbs", tags = ["script", "shell"])
tagged_file(name = "yae_tag", src = "yae", tags = ["script", "shell"])
tagged_file(name = "defn_tag", src = "defn", tags = ["clojure", "script"])
tagged_file(name = "k3k_tag", src = "k3k", tags = ["script", "shell"])
tagged_file(name = "other_tag", src = "other", tags = ["clojure", "script"])

[fmt_test(
    name = "dispatch_cue_fmt",
    src = src,
    tool = "cue",
) for src in glob(["dispatch.cue"], allow_empty = True)]

[tagged_file(
    name = "dispatch_cue_tag",
    src = src,
    tags = ["cue", "source"],
) for src in glob(["dispatch.cue"], allow_empty = True)]

bazel-runner.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# bazel-runner -- run bazelisk with a normalized environment to preserve
# the Bazel analysis cache across tool version changes.
#
# Bazel's server hashes the client environment to decide whether to reuse
# the analysis cache. Mise activates versioned tool paths (e.g.,
# .../installs/go/1.26.2/bin) which change on every upgrade, invalidating
# the cache even when the build graph is identical.
#
# This wrapper strips the environment down to a stable minimum before
# invoking bazelisk, so the analysis cache survives tool upgrades.
set -euo pipefail

# Resolve paths from the actual environment, not a hardcoded dev user. On
# the canonical devcontainer (HOME=/home/ubuntu, repo at $HOME/m) these
# resolve to the same literal strings the old hardcoded values had, so the
# Bazel analysis cache key is unchanged there; on any other machine/user
# (CI's `runner`, a lifted fork) they resolve correctly. The cache is
# per-machine (per output_base) anyway; the stability that matters is "no
# VERSIONED tool dirs in PATH", which still holds (only mise shims, which
# are version-independent). See AIDR-00149.
SELF_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
HOME_DIR="${HOME:?HOME must be set}"
SHIMS_DIR="${MISE_DATA_DIR:-$HOME_DIR/.local/share/mise}/shims"

# Stable PATH: this bin/ (for bbs shebang), mise shims (version-independent
# tool resolution), and system paths. No versioned tool directories.
STABLE_PATH="$SELF_DIR:$SHIMS_DIR:$HOME_DIR/.local/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin"

# Resolve bazelisk binary via mise shims (stable path, not versioned)
BAZELISK="$SHIMS_DIR/bazelisk"

# Run bazelisk with a minimal, stable environment.
# Only pass vars that Bazel or its toolchains actually need.
exec env -i \
	HOME="$HOME_DIR" \
	USER="${USER:-$(id -un)}" \
	PATH="$STABLE_PATH" \
	TERM="${TERM:-xterm-256color}" \
	"$BAZELISK" "$@"

bbs.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# bbs - babashka shebang wrapper with reader macro support
#
# Usage as shebang: #!/usr/bin/env bbs
#
# Features:
#   - Adds m/lib/ to classpath for shared libraries
#   - Supports #MISE reader tag for mise task annotations
#   - Resolves lib/ via git (preferred) or relative to own location (Bazel sandbox)
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT="${1:-}"
if [[ -z "$SCRIPT" ]]; then
	exec bb "$@"
fi
shift

# Resolve lib dir in order of preference:
# 1. BBS_LIB env var (set by Bazel BUILD files for sandbox)
# 2. git repo relative path (normal dev usage)
# 3. relative to this script's location (fallback)
if [[ -n "${BBS_LIB:-}" ]]; then
	# BBS_LIB can point to a file (e.g. Bazel rootpath) or a directory.
	if [[ -d "$BBS_LIB" ]]; then
		LIB_DIR="$BBS_LIB"
	else
		LIB_DIR="$(dirname "$BBS_LIB")"
	fi
elif git rev-parse --show-cdup >/dev/null 2>&1; then
	CDUP="$(git rev-parse --show-cdup)"
	LIB_DIR="${CDUP}m/kernel/lib"
else
	SELF_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
	LIB_DIR="${SELF_DIR}/../kernel/lib"
fi

exec bb -cp "${LIB_DIR}" \
	-e "(set! *data-readers* {'MISE (constantly nil)}) (load-file \"${SCRIPT}\")" \
	-- "$@"

bootstrap-bazelrc.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# bootstrap-bazelrc -- write m/.bazelrc.workspace from the workspace's
# actual on-disk paths. Run once after cloning (and re-run if your
# workspace moves on disk).
#
# Why: m/.bazelrc has path-dependent settings (HOME,
# MISE_TRUSTED_CONFIG_PATHS, PATH entries pointing at m/bin,
# sandbox_writable_path for ~/.cache) that Bazel can't substitute
# from %workspace% at action-env evaluation time. Pre-AIDR-00071
# follow-up these paths were hardcoded to /home/ubuntu/m, breaking
# any fork at a different path. This script reads $HOME and the
# git workspace root, then writes the path-dependent lines into
# .bazelrc.workspace which is gitignored and try-imported by
# .bazelrc.
#
# Has zero dependencies (pure bash + git), so it runs on a fresh
# clone before mise/bazel are available.

set -euo pipefail

# Locate the m/ workspace root: this script lives at m/bin/, so
# the m/ dir is its parent. Resolve symlinks for stability.
SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd -P)
WORKSPACE=$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd -P)
HOME_DIR=${HOME:?HOME must be set}

# On macOS the Linux-style /home/ubuntu path (used by the
# devcontainer) is symlinked from /Users/<user>; pick whichever
# the workspace lives under so the rendered paths match what
# Bazel sees.
case "$WORKSPACE" in
/home/*) ;;
/Users/*) ;;
*) ;; # fork at /tmp/foo/m or wherever -- use as-is
esac

OUT="$WORKSPACE/.bazelrc.workspace"

cat >"$OUT" <<EOF
# Generated by m/bin/bootstrap-bazelrc -- DO NOT EDIT.
# Run m/bin/bootstrap-bazelrc to refresh after moving the workspace.
#
# Path-dependent action_env / test_env / sandbox flags. Loaded by
# .bazelrc via try-import. Per-clone; gitignored. See AIDR-00071
# follow-up for the kernel/tenant decoupling design that motivates
# the workspace-relative bazelrc.

build --sandbox_writable_path=$HOME_DIR/.cache
# mise writes a per-config tracking symlink under
# XDG_STATE_HOME/mise/tracked-configs on every invocation; allow
# that path so it doesn't warn from inside the sandbox.
build --sandbox_writable_path=$HOME_DIR/.local/state/mise

build --action_env=HOME=$HOME_DIR
build --action_env=MISE_CONFIG_FILE=$WORKSPACE/mise.toml
build --action_env=MISE_TRUSTED_CONFIG_PATHS=$WORKSPACE/mise.toml
build --action_env=PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/coreutils/libexec/gnubin:/opt/homebrew/bin:$WORKSPACE/bin:$HOME_DIR/.local/share/mise/shims:$HOME_DIR/.local/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin

build --test_env=HOME=$HOME_DIR
build --test_env=MISE_CONFIG_FILE=$WORKSPACE/mise.toml
build --test_env=MISE_TRUSTED_CONFIG_PATHS=$WORKSPACE/mise.toml
build --test_env=PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/coreutils/libexec/gnubin:/opt/homebrew/bin:$WORKSPACE/bin:$HOME_DIR/.local/share/mise/shims:$HOME_DIR/.local/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin
EOF

echo "wrote $OUT (workspace=$WORKSPACE, home=$HOME_DIR)"

defn.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bbs
;; bin/defn -- freshness-guaranteed shim that delegates to
;; `mise run defn`, which rebuilds via bazel before exec-ing.
;;
;; Background:
;; This path used to be a 138MB compiled binary, kept up-to-date
;; only when someone remembered to run `mise run build-go`.
;; That manual step let the artifact silently fall behind source
;; (twice during AIDR-00135 development the stale binary masked
;; a real behavior change for hours).
;;
;; The shim is babashka per AIREF-00011 (no plain bash scripts).
;; Bazel's incremental cache keeps the no-op rebuild path sub-
;; second, so the freshness guarantee is effectively free.
;;
;; Args flow through to the CLI verbatim:
;;   bin/defn dispatch --bricks=app--keda --worktree ...
;; is equivalent to
;;   mise run defn -- dispatch --bricks=app--keda --worktree ...

(require '[babashka.process :as p])

(p/exec (into ["mise" "run" "defn" "--"] *command-line-args*))

k3k.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# k3k -- shim that exposes rancher/k3k's k3kcli on PATH as `k3k`.
#
# ubi (mise's ubi backend) installs raw-binary github releases under
# the asset's filename, not the repo basename, so the rancher/k3k
# release lands as `k3kcli`. This wrapper lets operators type `k3k`
# the way they'd type any other CLI without learning that the asset
# name and the user-facing verb differ. Pinned via the project's
# mise.toml (mise x resolves the configured version).
#
# AIDR-00129 Option C; closes the m/bin/k3k slot in the
# Stamper-post-tenant-split TODO.
set -euo pipefail

exec mise x github:rancher/k3k -- k3kcli "$@"

other.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bbs
;; bin/other -- fork namesake CLI shim (AIDR-00141 Stage 3.5d).
;; Delegates to `mise run defn`, which reads default_tenant from the
;; lattice and builds //tenant/<t>/go/cmd/<t>:<t> -- the same task
;; serves every tenant's namesake CLI.

(require '[babashka.process :as p])

(p/exec (into ["mise" "run" "defn" "--"] *command-line-args*))

yae.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# yae - yaegi shebang wrapper
#
# Usage as shebang: #!/usr/bin/env yae
#
# Features:
#   - Strips leading #-prefixed lines (shebang, #MISE annotations) so
#     yaegi receives valid Go source; mise still reads #MISE metadata
#     from the original file before executing
#   - Falls through to `yaegi` for direct invocation when no script given
#
# Args note: yaegi sets os.Args to the full interpreter invocation, so
# in a script `os.Args` is [yaegi, run, tmpfile, --, ...user-args].
# To extract user args, scan os.Args for "--" and take everything after it.
set -euo pipefail

# TODO: add a syntax/type-check mode (pre-execution go build) for yae scripts.

SCRIPT="${1:-}"
if [[ -z "$SCRIPT" ]]; then
	exec yaegi "$@"
fi
shift

TMP=$(mktemp /tmp/yae_XXXXXX.go)
grep -v '^#' "$SCRIPT" >"$TMP"
yaegi run -unrestricted "$TMP" -- "$@"
STATUS=$?
rm -f "$TMP"
exit $STATUS
